text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Think of it like reorganizing a messy room—everything remains in the same place functionally, but it becomes easier to navigate. The main goal is to enhance readability and maintainability, making the code simpler to understand and modify in the future. Refactoring also helps reduce technical debt, which is the extra work required to fix poorly written code over time. By regularly refactoring, developers can keep a codebase clean, efficient, and adaptable to new requirements, ultimately saving time and effort.
